iterations.command:
python train.py --epochs 2 --batch_size 32 --num_workers 6 --save_path checkpoints/baseline.pt && python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
import shutil
root=Path('/rab_data/asvspoof2021_la/dev')
out=Path('tmp_pred_data')
(out/'flac').mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
ids=[]
for p in sorted((root/'flac').glob('*.flac'))[:64]:
    ids.append(p.stem)
    shutil.copy2(p, out/'flac'/p.name)
with open(out/'test.txt','w') as f:
    f.write('\n'.join(ids)+'\n')
print('prepared', len(ids))
PY
python predict.py --data_path tmp_pred_data --checkpoint checkpoints/baseline.pt --output tmp_pred.json --batch_size 32 --num_workers 2 && python - <<'PY'
import json
x=json.load(open('tmp_pred.json'))
print('num_preds', len(x['predictions']), 'first', x['predictions'][0])
PY

iterations.description: Observation: dataset discovery now works and training starts, but the collator crashes because this feature extractor does not return attention_mask by default. Analysis: wav2vec2 feature extractors often need return_attention_mask=True at load time/call time, and we should robustly handle missing masks by creating all-ones masks. Also train.py appears truncated in workspace, so safest is to rewrite complete train.py and align predict.py with the same mask fallback. Decision: patch train/predict collators to request and synthesize attention masks, keep the short 2-epoch baseline, test predict locally on dev subset, then submit eval immediately if successful.

iterations.description: Observation: training and local predict test succeeded with dev EER 0.034362, but evaluation failed because predict.py assumed test.txt is directly under --data_path while the eval harness passed /data and the actual test bundle is likely nested. Analysis: the main blocker is path resolution in predict.py, not model quality. We should make predict.py robust to nested dataset roots and also suppress repeated transformer load noise by loading local feature extractor first. Since the baseline is already trained and local inference works, the fastest next step is to patch predict.py, verify it still works locally, and resubmit the same checkpoint to get the first official score and runtime. Decision: update predict.py with recursive test-root discovery and local feature-extractor fallback, run a local smoke test, then submit eval with the existing checkpoint.

iterations.description: Observation: baseline checkpoint is trained and local smoke test passes, but official eval still fails because predict.py cannot resolve the eval test bundle when --data_path is /data. Analysis: the issue is path discovery robustness in predict.py, likely because the harness mounts the actual test directory deeper than one level or under a symlinked path. The current resolver is too narrow and the file may also be truncated in workspace, so safest is to rewrite predict.py completely with aggressive recursive discovery for test.txt/flac, plus support for the case where --data_path itself is the flac dir or where test.txt is elsewhere under the tree. Decision: fully rewrite predict.py, keep model/checkpoint format unchanged, run a local smoke test against tmp_pred_data, then resubmit the existing best checkpoint immediately to obtain the first official score before doing more training.

iterations.description: Observation: official eval now reaches dataloader/audio reading, so path resolution is fixed; the remaining failure is soundfile.LibsndfileError during predict-time loading in worker processes. Analysis: this is likely due to brittle FLAC decoding in multiprocessing or a single problematic file causing worker crash; the safest fix is to make predict.py robust by avoiding multi-worker decode, adding fallback audio loading via torchaudio/librosa, and fully rewriting the truncated file so offline inference is deterministic. Decision: rewrite predict.py completely with resilient test-root discovery, single-process loading, decode fallback, length bucketing, and local-only artifact loading; verify locally on tmp_pred_data and resubmit the existing checkpoint immediately to get the first official score before more training.

iterations.command: python train.py --epochs 4 --batch_size 24 --num_workers 6 --checkpoint checkpoints/xlsr_ft.pt && python predict.py --data_path tmp_pred_data --checkpoint checkpoints/xlsr_ft.pt --output tmp_pred.json
iterations.description: Observation: phase-1 prediction now completes and the remaining failure is evaluator-side missing hidden labels, so the pipeline is likely valid. Analysis: we still have no official metric because the harness is broken, but we can use the remaining budget to improve the model. The current baseline already achieved strong local dev EER (~0.034), and the most promising next gain is to train longer with a stronger encoder while keeping predict.py/checkpoint compatibility robust. Decision: rewrite full non-truncated model/train/predict files, switch to wav2vec2-xls-r-300m with partial fine-tuning of top layers, balanced BCE, length bucketing, and save a new checkpoint; also keep offline-safe local feature extractor loading and test predict locally before future eval.
